The massively influential and highly cartelized Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC) formally convened in Vienna for a massive, highly contentious, and globally scrutinized summit. ## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On September 11, 1973, an incredibly massive, absolutely brutal, and historically defining military coup d'état violently erupted in Chile. General Augusto Pinochet, heavily backed by the United States CIA, aggressively and violently overthrew the massive, democratically elected socialist government of President Salvador Allende. As heavily armed fighter jets aggressively bombed the La Moneda presidential palace, Allende committed suicide, instantly plunging the massive South American nation into a horrific, 17-year absolute military dictatorship defined by massive torture and thousands of "disappeared" dissidents.

On September 11, 2001 (Historical context note), the absolute, most incredibly massive, terrifying, and globally defining act of international terrorism in human history violently occurred. Nineteen Al-Qaeda terrorists aggressively hijacked four massive commercial airliners. Two were aggressively flown into the Twin Towers of the World Trade Center in New York, and one into the massive Pentagon in Arlington, Virginia. The horrific, massive devastation resulted in 2,977 tragic deaths, fundamentally shattering global security architecture and aggressively launching the massive, multi-decade Global War on Terror.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On September 11, 1893, Swami Vivekananda delivered his incredibly massive, historically immortal, and globally defining address at the Parliament of the World's Religions in Chicago. Beginning his massive speech with the incredibly iconic words, "Sisters and brothers of America," he aggressively commanded an absolute, massive standing ovation. His incredibly brilliant, massive articulation of Vedanta philosophy and Hindu tolerance fundamentally revolutionized massive Western perceptions of Indian spirituality, cementing his legacy as an absolute, towering cultural ambassador.

On September 11, 1906, Mahatma Gandhi officially and aggressively launched his absolute, foundational philosophy of *Satyagraha* (truth-force or non-violent resistance) in South Africa. At a massive, historic gathering at the Empire Theatre in Johannesburg, Gandhi fiercely organized the massive Indian diaspora to aggressively refuse compliance with the deeply oppressive, massive Asiatic Registration Act (the Black Act). This incredibly massive, uncompromising moment of civil disobedience heavily laid the absolute, architectural bedrock for the massive, successful Indian independence movement decades later.
